title: draba moupinensis var. calcicola
intro: Draba moupinensis var. calcicola is a rare and lesser-known succulent plant species, belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It is also known as the calcicolous mountain draba, which is characterized by its small, white flowers and thick, fleshy leaves. In traditional medicine, the plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and poultices, and is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
